families.name: Sousa
history:
When we talk about this family history, it is possible to be affirmative about the relationship with nobility () and also the Santa Maria de Pombeiro monastery (). Now when we are going to talk about the family origin, we have distinct versions. According to the material studied, we observed that:
  • José Mattoso's propose, that indicates a document from Pombeiro as 'apocryphal', it is the one nearest the facts ().
  • Medieval Lands starts the linage with Mendo Veegas de Sousa (), but when they also cite his parents.
  • The Medieval Lands follow the "Livro Velho de Linhagens I" that presents Egas de Sousa marriage with Gontinha Gonçalves (), parents of Mendo Veegas de Sousa as the first Sousa, this fact is reinforced for António D'Almeida ().
  • The "Livro Velho de Linhagens II" starts family history with Uffo Belfages, that is the hypothetical father of Conde Guiçoi ().
  • The count Don Pedro adds more generations to Sousa starting the linage with Suero Belfages ().
  • The last extension to Sousa's history studied until now was made for Frey Bernaro de Britto, in his work Frey starts the family with Faião Soares ().
Considering all these possibilities, we are going to threat as hypothesis all people  without documentation.

people.name: Faião Soares
history: The Britto's hypothesis (<htcite0>) was correctly explained for Almeida (<htcite1>), considering that nothing is necessary to add, I am finishing it here.

Gaio works with Britto's propose, but he also suggests that Sueiro Belfager could have another filiation without giving any proofs (<htcite2>). The Francisco X. Garcia work brings additional details about his life (<htcite3>).

children.name: Sueiro Belfager
history: According to Fray Bernardo de Britto (<htcite0>).

people.name: Soeiro Belfager
history: It was not found any document proofing this person's existence, so we are showing here as nobiliarchy suggestion.

The count Don Pedro starts the Sousa history with  him (<htcite0>). After a long explanation about his hypothetical father, Friar Britto does not give a lot of information about him (<htcite1>). Garcia's compilation about Soeiro brings more details about him and his supposed wife (<htcite2>). I am ending this explanation bringing Gaio's suggestions, he cites Britto initial conclusion, but he also gives another possibility without proof (<htcite3>).

marriages.name: Menaia Ribeiro
history: According to Don Pedro (<htcite0>).

source.text: Sousa, Manuel de F. et al., 1646, Dom Pedro

children.name: Hufo Soares
history: According to Don Pedro (<htcite0>).

source.text: Sousa, Manuel de F. et al., 1646, Dom Pedro
Sousa, Manuel de F., et al., 1646, Dom Pedro

people.name: Hufo Soares
history: According to Gaio (<htcite0>) would be a person that confirmed a document during 'Afonso III, o magno', years. When Garcia worked with these events, he gave us more details (<htcite1>). Garcia also added that his Latin name 'Aufo' was modified to 'Ataulfo' and that he signed the document with his 'first cousin' Ermenegildo (<htcite2>). These details and citation present on the same page conduct us to the document inside Britto's work (<htcite3>), but the document date is incompatible with reported facts, this scenario conducts us to consider this person as another hypothesis.
Don Pedro cites his with a Latin name (<htcite4>).

marriages.name: Omendola
history: According to Don Pedro (<htcite0>).

source.text: Sousa, Manuel de F. et al., 1646, Dom Pedro

children.name: Hufo Hufes
history: According to Don Pedro (<htcite0>).

source.text: Sousa, Manuel de F. et al., 1646, Dom Pedro
Sousa, Manuel de F. et al., 1646, Dom Pedro

people.name: Hufo Hufes
history: Don Pedro cites him with a Latin name 'Ahufo Ahufes' (<htcite0>). Britto adds a document that he got from Arouca, this document was written during the year of 925 A.D./Era 963 (<htcite1>), but this document brings the name used here, Hufo Hufes.

The book "Livro Velho de Linhagens II" starts the Sousa history with Uffo Belfages (<htcite2>). When we take a look only at his name, we could associate the person with Hufo Soares already described, but we would have an anachronism with Don Pedro's hypothesis. Considering the contradictions present in different nobiliarchies, we decided to describe these contradictions here.

marriages.name: Teresa
history: According to Don Pedro (<htcite0>).

When Garcia brings details about Teresa without presenting primary sources, he gave the information that she was Terez Eriz, daughter of count Ero Fernandes (<htcite1>), but this information probably is wrong, because a document written during the year of 956 A.D./Era 994 give us the information that Tereza Eris was in marriage with Gonzalo Betotis (<htcite2>).

children.name: Senhorinha
history: According to Don Pedro (<htcite0>), that did not give us a lot of details, but Lavaña added details that she passed away during ihe year of 972 (<htcite1>). Britto o acompanha (<htcite2>).

source.text: Sousa, Manuel de F. et al., 1646, Dom Pedro
Sousa, Manuel de F. et al., 1646, Dom Pedro

children.name: Gonçalo Soares
history: According to Britto (<htcite0>), this is not cited in nobiliarchies, but Britto says that he is the count Don Gozoy cited for Don Pedro (<htcite1>). This hypothesis has the issue to break completely the patronymic for previous and next generation, that has Echiguez Vizoi as his son, when he would be in the better scenario a grandson.

source.text: Sousa, Manuel de F. et al., 1646, Dom Pedro

children.name: Vizoy Vizos
history: According to Garcia's compilation (<htcite0>).

children.name: Vizoe Hufes
history:
I only found this hypothesis on Roglo website (). It is bringing here, because whether it is proofed real, it could address two issues:
  • The patronymic issue would not exist anymore.
  • Senhorinha passed away during the year of 972 according to Lavaña (), and his hypothetical father signed a document during the year of 925 A.D./Era 963 (I am considering that original document had Hispânia epoch that was used during the time it was written.). According to this scenario, we had to accept that their father had a short life, or he was not their father, so to add this generation could address this issue.

I end this description giving the information that I am using Roglo instead of the researcher Manuel Abranches de Soveral, because in an e-mail sent to him, I was informed that an error present on the website was not made for himself, but his name was on the website together other researchers.

source.text: Sousa, Manuel de F. et al., 1646, Dom Pedro
Herculano, Alexandre (1860), Scriptores, Volvmen I, Fascicuvuls II

people.name: Vizoy Vizois
history: Everybody studied until now for this family had the issue of having missing documentation or facts that are utopia around them, but when we talk about Vizoy we have a different scenario. This is a person documented during the year of 985 A.D./Era 1023 (<htcite0>; <htcite1>) and he is also cited in a donation made for Paio Nunes that names him as his great-grandparent (<htcite2>; <htcite3>).
The possible confusions related with his person in nobiliarchies were described in his parents' generation, so I will only add here that Gaio cited him as Gozoy and Vizoy (<htcite4>).

children.name: Égica Vizois
history: See next generation for more details.

people.name: Égica Vizois
history: There is not any documentation available nowadays that allows us to confirm this person (<htcite0>), so we accept his existence, because his [hypothetical] son has the patronymic Echiguez, and because the Paio Nunes' document give us information about a number of generations trough count Guizoi that forces another person here (<htcite1>). It would exist another doucument published during the year of 1959 by the magazine Douro Litoral that also brings suppose information about him (<htcite2>), but it was not possible to check the whole paper.

marriages.name: Aragunta Gonçalves
history: According to A. A. Fernandes (<htcite0>).

marriages.name: Aragunta Soares
history: According to Livro Velho de Linhagens II (<htcite0>) followed by other references already cited.

source.text: Herculano, Alexandre (1860), Scriptores, Volvmen I, Fascicuvuls II

children.name: Gomes Echiguez
history: According José Mattoso (<htcite0>).

source.text: Douro-Litoral da Comissão Provincial de Etnografia e História, 1959

people.name: Gomes Echiguez
history: He is documented between 1049 A.D./Era 1087 and 1072 A.D./Era 1110 (<htcite0>), he is cited one of Portuguese's 'Infanções' together his [hypothetical] grandfather (<htcite1>), he was named as Gomes Egirz in a scripture of king Fernando (<htcite2>; <htcite3>).

Gaio treats him as Don and as a person responsible for distinct exploits that allowed him to merge with a hypothetical granddaughter  of a king (<htcite4>), there is no documentation giving him any noble title (<htcite5>).

marriages.name: Gontrode Moniz
history: Different Portuguese's nobiliarchies cite her giving not real ancestry (<htcite0>; <htcite1>; <htcite2>).

source.text: Herculano, Alexandre (1860), Scriptores, Volvmen I, Fascicuvuls II
Sousa, Manuel de F. et al., 1646, Dom Pedro

children.name: Egas Gomes
history: Please, take a look in his generation.

people.name: Egas Gomes
history: According to nobiliarchies (<htcite0>; <htcite1>), because there is not any available documentation where he appears, these documents probably were lost (<htcite2>). The document cited previously (<htcite3>) also talks about him, on the other hand, it is possible to observe cronicles centuries after the supposed events describing big histories where Egas was the main protagonist (<htcite4>).

marriages.name: Gontinha Gonçalves
history: Probably she was part of Maya's family (<htcite0>; <htcite1>; <htcite2>), but if António de A. Fernandes is correct, so he was married to his grandaunt.

children.name: Mendo Veegas

people.name: Mendo Veegas
history: José Mattoso calls attention to the fact that the family of Sousa starts its ascension with this person (<htcite0>). His presence in documentations is observed since Don Raimundo's time during the year of 1094 A.D./ Era 1132 (<htcite1>) until Dona Teresa's time (<htcite2>).

marriages.name: Teresa Fernandes
history: According to Livro Velho I (<htcite0>) and confirmed by a document in Pedroso Monastery (<htcite1>).

marriages.name: Elvira Fernandes
history: According to Don Pedro (<htcite0>), but this is not possible, because a document in Pedroso Monastery shows this is incorrect (<htcite1>).
